content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 15, 1994

Anne Magnie
550 Cleveland Avenue
Sebastopol, CA  95472

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-94-245

Dear Ms. Magnie:

     You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/  Since your request is one for general
information, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

     Your letter dated August 1, 1994, correctly summarizes my
advice.  Based on the information you have provided, the City of
Sebastopol's campaign ordinance does not place limits on campaign
contributions, and the candidates are not running in a special
election.  Thus the transfer ban of Proposition 73 does not apply.
Because the transfer ban does not apply, three city council
candidates involved in a single fundraising event are not
restricted from transferring funds and reimbursing each other's
committees for expenses incurred in connection with the event.  If
the proceeds of the event will be disbursed between the
candidates, it will be necessary to notify the contributors of
this intent.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant

_____________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6, of the California Code of Regulations.

2/  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

sections.conclusion: Based on the information you have provided, the City of Sebastopol's campaign ordinance does not place limits on campaign contributions, and the candidates are not running in a special election. Thus the transfer ban of Proposition 73 does not apply. Because the transfer ban does not apply, three city council candidates involved in a single fundraising event are not restricted from transferring funds and reimbursing each other's committees for expenses incurred in connection with the event. If the proceeds of the event will be disbursed between the candidates, it will be necessary to notify the contributors of this intent.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the transfer ban of Proposition 73 applies to three city council candidates in Sebastopol who are conducting a joint fundraising event and seeking to transfer funds and reimburse each other's committees for event expenses.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Proposition 73 transfer ban does not apply because Sebastopol's ordinance lacks contribution limits and the candidates are not in a special election. Therefore, the three city council candidates may transfer funds and reimburse each other's committee expenses from the joint fundraising event, provided contributors are notified of the fund disbursement intent.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming prior telephone advice. No formal Q/A section headers, but question and conclusion are clearly present in body text. Document includes disclaimer that informal assistance does not provide immunity.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the transfer ban of Proposition 73 applies to three city council candidates in Sebastopol who are conducting a joint fundraising event and seeking to transfer funds and reimburse each other's committees for event expenses.

CONCLUSION: Based on the information you have provided, the City of Sebastopol's campaign ordinance does not place limits on campaign contributions, and the candidates are not running in a special election. Thus the transfer ban of Proposition 73 does not apply. Because the transfer ban does not apply, three city council candidates involved in a single fundraising event are not restricted from transferring funds and reimbursing each other's committees for expenses incurred in connection with the event. If the proceeds of the event will be disbursed between the candidates, it will be necessary to notify the contributors of this intent.

Based on the information you have provided, the City of Sebastopol's campaign ordinance does not place limits on campaign contributions, and the candidates are not running in a special election. Thus the transfer ban of Proposition 73 does not apply. Because the transfer ban does not apply, three city council candidates involved in a single fundraising event are not restricted from transferring funds and reimbursing each other's committees for expenses incurred in connection with the event. If the proceeds of the event will be disbursed between the candidates, it will be necessary to notify the contributors of this intent. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms informal telephone advice that Proposition 73's transfer ban does not apply to three Sebastopol city council candidates conducting a joint fundraising event, allowing them to transfer funds and reimburse each other's expenses provided contributors are notified.
